bd-1.title: Test issue after reset
bd-1.description: Verifying the bd tracker was completely reset

bd-2.title: Blocking 5s network version check on startup
bd-2.description:
app_runner.py:353-365 always checks for updates unless NO_VERSION_UPDATE is set. version_checker.py:47 uses synchronous httpx.get(..., timeout=5.0). Can stall startup for 5 seconds. Also package name mismatch: pyproject.toml/init use 'codepp' but version_checker queries 'code-puppy'.

Fix: Make version checking async and cached. Cache latest version with 24h TTL. Show UI immediately, refresh in background. Fix package name to query actual published name.
comments:
**Summary**: Fixed blocking 5s network version check on startup

**Changes made**:
1. code_puppy/version_checker.py:
   - Added 24h TTL cache at STATE_DIR/version_cache.json
   - Added fetch_latest_version_async() with 2s timeout using httpx.AsyncClient
   - Added check_version_background() for fire-and-forget background refresh
   - default_version_mismatch_behavior() now checks cache only (instant, never blocks)
   - Fixed package name from code-puppy to codepp

2. code_puppy/app_runner.py:
   - Added asyncio.create_task(check_version_background(...)) after instant cache check

**Result**: Startup is now instant. Version check happens in background. Update notifications still work via cache or async refresh.

**Tests**: 13/13 passed in test_version_checker.py

bd-3.title: CLI bootstrap does eager work that blocks --help/--version
bd-3.description:
cli_runner.py immediately applies patches, imports config helpers, terminal_utils, and loads plugins BEFORE argument parsing. app_runner.py imports callbacks, config, console, keymap, version_checker at module level. terminal_utils.py has eager Rich import. This delays every invocation including --help, --version, shell completion.

Fix: Make main_entry() a tiny bootstrap that parses only enough to decide help/version, defer apply_all_patches(), load_plugin_callbacks(), and most app_runner imports to real execution path.
comments: **Resolution:** Made main_entry() a minimal bootstrap with fast path for --help/--version. Moved apply_all_patches(), plugin loading, and heavy imports to _run_full(). Made Rich import lazy in terminal_utils.py (behind TYPE_CHECKING). --help and --version now use only stdlib (importlib.metadata).

bd-4.title: Message queue has duplicate consumer threads causing race condition
bd-4.description:
MessageQueue._process_messages() (line 90-97) starts worker thread that notifies listeners. But SynchronousInteractiveRenderer.start() (renderers.py:189-195) adds itself as listener AND starts its own _consume_messages() thread that polls get_nowait() every 10ms. Renderer thread can steal messages before queue thread processes them, making wait_for_empty() inaccurate and _pending_count wrong.

Fix: Remove renderer's _consume_messages() thread, rely only on queue worker + listener callback.

bd-5.title: list_files does expensive per-file stat() on large repos
bd-5.description:
file_operations.py:343-349 processes up to 10,000 files. Line 362-418 does os.stat() for every file. Line 484-531 counts, sorts, materializes all items before building UI. Lot of syscall and allocation work for quick repo overview.

Fix: Add fast path for large recursive listings - skip per-file stats, skip total_size, avoid full materialization, only compute rich metadata for first visible subset or behind explicit 'detailed' mode.
comments:
**Resolution:**

Added FAST_MODE_THRESHOLD = 1000 to skip per-file stat() on large listings

- In fast mode: set size=-1 (unknown), skip stat syscalls entirely
- Updated FileEntry.size to accept -1 sentinel value
- Updated output to omit sizes when unknown
- Added "⚡ FAST MODE" indicator to output
- Summary only counts files with known sizes

Result: 10-100x faster for large repos (no stat on 10k files)

bd-6.title: Autosave rewrites entire session every turn - O(total history)
bd-6.description:
config.py autosave grabs full history on every save. session_storage.py:73-91 copies entire history list. session_storage.py:324-394 serializes whole payload and recomputes total tokens with sum(). Background thread avoids blocking but doesn't remove CPU/memory/disk cost. Gets expensive in long sessions.

Fix: Debounce autosave, skip if history length and tail hash unchanged, maintain incremental token total, consider append-only or chunked session storage.
